item_text: At least some lobbying data available (in any format) either by request (email, telephone, fax, etc.) or anonymously (web-based).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if ANY disclosure data is obtainable by any means; 0 only if the state has no accessible disclosure data at all.

item_text: The state has a dedicated website for lobbying information.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a state-operated website hosts lobbying information. Likely 1 for all 50 states in 2026 — retained for continuity with PRI 2010.

item_text: The state's lobbying website can be found via a plain search (e.g., first-page Google result for "<state> lobbying disclosure").
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the official state portal appears in the first 5 organic Google results for the query; 0 otherwise. Document the search URL and result position as evidence.
notes: Sharpened 2026 scoring guidance — PRI 2010 left ""easily"" undefined.

item_text: At least some current lobbying data/information available on the website for the most recent year.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if filings from the current calendar year are viewable on the portal; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Historical lobbying information is available, downloadable, or at least viewable going back at least 5 years.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if filings from at least 5 prior years are accessible on the portal; 0 otherwise. Document the earliest viewable year as evidence.
notes: Sharpened 2026 scoring guidance — PRI 2010 did not specify horizon; 5 years chosen as a research-useful floor.

item_text: Users can download lobbying data in a structured electronic format (CSV, Excel, XML, JSON) immediately usable for analysis — as opposed to only HTML tables, PDFs, or image-based scans.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if at least one download path yields a structured, parseable format (CSV/Excel/XML/JSON). Q10 (bulk download) and Q11 (API) capture stronger variants.
notes: 2026 guidance clarifies ""electronic format"" vs PRI 2010's ""Excel or SPSS"".

item_text: Can users specify data searches on the portal by principal (city; authority hiring; employer; corporation).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal's search interface accepts principal-name or principal-entity as a filter field.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by principal's location (address; city).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal's search interface accepts principal address or city as a filter.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by lobbyist name.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal's search interface accepts lobbyist name as a filter.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by lobbyist location (address; city).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal's search interface accepts lobbyist address or city as a filter.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by specific date.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal's search interface accepts a specific date as a filter.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by specific time period (quarter or state-specific filing interval).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal's search interface accepts a reporting-period range as a filter.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by total expenditures.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by a total-spend threshold or range.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by compensation spending.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by compensation amount specifically.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by miscellaneous expenses (non-compensation).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by non-compensation expense amount.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by sources of funding (public/taxpayer-funded emphasis).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter records by funding-source category.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by subject of lobbying (or item of legislation).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by subject/topic or legislation.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by designated entities (official/body the lobbyist is engaging).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by target entity (committee; agency; legislator).

item_text: Can users specify data searches by legal status of the principal (government; non-profit; for-profit).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by principal legal-status category.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by sector (transport; energy; banking; education; etc.).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by sector.

item_text: Can users specify data searches by sub-sector (K–12; secondary; vocational; etc.).
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if users can filter by sub-sector.

item_text: Users can simultaneously sort lobbying data by multiple criteria (combine 2+ Q7 filters in a single query).
scoring_guidance: Raw score = count of Q7 sub-criteria that can be combined in a single query (0–15). Normalize by /15 to produce 0–1 sub-score at scoring time.
notes: PRI 2010 never specified how to arrive at the 0–15 raw score; 2026 guidance = count of Q7 sub-criteria combinable in one search.

item_text: For Q7a filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a filter-by-principal search returns a downloadable result set (not just a viewable page).

category: Download sort results — by principal location
item_text: For Q7b filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a filter-by-principal-location search returns a downloadable result set.

category: Download sort results — by lobbyist name

scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a filter-by-lobbyist-name search returns a downloadable result set.

scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a funding-source-filtered search returns a downloadable result set.

item_text: For Q7k filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a subject-filtered search returns a downloadable result set.

category: Download sort results — by designated entity
item_text: For Q7l filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a designated-entity-filtered search returns a downloadable result set.

category: Download sort results — by legal status
item_text: For Q7m filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a legal-status-filtered search returns a downloadable result set.

item_text: For Q7n filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a sector-filtered search returns a downloadable result set.

item_text: For Q7o filter results, can the filtered/sorted dataset be downloaded?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a sub-sector-filtered search returns a downloadable result set.

item_text: The complete dataset (all lobbying disclosures for a time range) is downloadable as a single file or small set of files — not just per-record or per-search.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal offers a bulk export (e.g., "download all 2025 filings" as one/few CSV/JSON files). Per-record PDF downloads do NOT satisfy Q10; neither does a per-search download (Q9). Evidence = URL of bulk-export link.
notes: Captures the ""can you actually get all the data"" dimension separate from per-search export (Q9).

item_text: Documented programmatic API is available for querying lobbying data without payment.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal publishes API documentation and access is free (free API keys or no key required). Paid-only APIs score 0. Undocumented JSON endpoints discovered by inspection score 0. Evidence = URL of API docs.
notes: Rate-limit severity recorded as a note but does not by itself zero out the score.

item_text: No portion of lobbying disclosure data is gated behind login; paywall; or attestation.
scoring_guidance: REVERSE-SCORED. Score 1 if ALL data is accessible without authentication. Score 0 if ANY disclosure data requires login; registration; payment; or click-through attestation. Evidence = URL of gated content (if score=0) or confirmation of open access (if score=1).
notes: Structurally analogous to reverse-scored B1/B2 in the disclosure-law rubric.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "lobbyist name" field (definition; type; format; allowed values)?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal or linked docs define this field's meaning and format. Inline column headers alone do NOT count.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "lobbyist address" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "lobbyist phone" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "principal name" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "principal address" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "principal phone" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

category: Data dictionary — principal business nature field
item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "nature of principal's business" (public/private) field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

category: Data dictionary — direct lobbying cost field
item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "direct lobbying cost (compensation)" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

category: Data dictionary — indirect lobbying cost field
item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "indirect lobbying cost (non-compensation)" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "other costs (gifts; entertainment; transportation; lodging)" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "general issues lobbied" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "specific bill number / legislation ID" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "contacts disclosed" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Does the portal publish a data dictionary documenting the "principal financial contributors" field?
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if defined in the data dictionary; 0 otherwise.

item_text: Each filing/record has a stable; machine-readable unique ID that persists across portal redesigns.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if records expose a canonical ID field (e.g., filing_id) that is stable and documented. IDs that are session-bound or change across portal updates score 0.
notes: Q13 sub-item; record-level identifier per user direction (machine-readable IDs folded into Q13).

item_text: Each individual filing/record has a stable canonical URL that survives portal redesigns.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if records are addressable at a stable URL documented or observably persistent. Session-token URLs or AJAX-only views score 0. Evidence = example URL.

item_text: The original filed documents (typically PDFs) are retrievable — not only the parsed/extracted field values.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the portal exposes the original filed document alongside or linked from the structured record. Evidence = URL of a raw filing.
notes: Important because parsing errors in portal extraction can silently corrupt downstream analysis.

item_text: The portal displays a visible ""last updated"" timestamp for the data.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if a "last updated" or "data current as of" timestamp is visible on the landing or data page. Evidence = URL and quoted timestamp.

item_text: The data is genuinely current — filings from the current reporting period (per the state's cadence) are present.
scoring_guidance: Score 1 if the most recent filing date observed is within one filing cadence of the score date (e.g., if state requires quarterly filing; most recent filing ≤90 days old). Document most-recent-filing date as evidence.
notes: Captures the failure mode where a portal claims to be up-to-date but actually hasn't ingested recent filings.
